sourceMovie.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit King Arthur: Legend of the Sword for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Guy Ritchie keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: DragonHeart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If King Arthur: Legend of the Sword clicked for you, DragonHeart (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after King Arthur: Legend of the Sword?
faq.answer: Try DragonHeart, King Arthur, Conan the Barbarian—each shares DNA with King Arthur: Legend of the Sword while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is King Arthur: Legend of the Sword?
faq.answer: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword (2017) is best described as Action, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is King Arthur: Legend of the Sword worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
